prompt: Write the opening paragraph of a children's book about a shy octopus who wants to make friends.
response: Deep beneath the shimmering waves, where sunlight danced in golden ribbons and coral castles bloomed in every color of the rainbow, lived a little octopus named Ollie. Ollie had eight soft, wiggly arms perfect for building sand sculptures, juggling seashells, and even tying the tiniest sailor’s knots—but he never showed them off. You see, Ollie was shy. While his classmates zoomed through the kelp forest playing tag or gathered in giggly groups to share sea-grape snacks, Ollie would curl up behind a giant clamshell, peeking out with just one curious eye. He *wanted* to join in—oh, how he wanted to!—but every time he tried to wiggle over and say hello, his arms would tangle with nerves, and his words would vanish like bubbles in the current.
